data.title: Ethereum NFT Trading Volume Surges Over 72% in 24 Hours
data.description:
Ethereum's NFT trading volume has seen a significant increase, reaching $3.934 million in the past 24 hours, according to ChainCatcher. This marks a 72.78% rise in activity. Notably, Bored Ape Yacht Club (BAYC) NFTs recorded a trading volume exceeding $1.45 million, with a 24-hour increase of 266.35%. Countyard NFTs also experienced a substantial rise, with trading volumes around $980,000, marking an 88.1% increase.

Furthermore, Ethereum's NFT trading volume has surpassed $55 million, significantly higher than the approximately $37.095 million recorded in March.

data.title: SpaceX IPO May Divert Liquidity from Bitcoin and Risk Assets
data.description: The anticipated $75 billion initial public offering (IPO) of SpaceX is expected to attract significant liquidity, potentially impacting Bitcoin and other risk assets. According to NS3.AI, the combined effect of potential IPOs from companies like OpenAI and Anthropic could result in a liquidity shift exceeding $240 billion by the end of the year.

data.title: AI TRENDS | DeepSeek-V4 Preview Version Launched and Open-Sourced
data.description: On April 24, DeepSeek-V4's preview version was officially launched and open-sourced. According to Jin10, Baidu's Qianfan platform under Baidu Intelligent Cloud has adapted Day0 to provide API services. DeepSeek-V4 features a long context with millions of tokens and is available in two versions: DeepSeek-V4-Pro and DeepSeek-V4-Flash. Currently, enterprise users and developers can directly access DeepSeek-V4-Pro through the Baidu Qianfan console or API, while DeepSeek-V4-Flash will soon be fully available.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Qt Group and Chengmai Technology Collaborate on Smart Cockpit Technology at Beijing Auto Show
data.description:
On April 25, Jin10 reported that the 19th Beijing International Automotive Exhibition commenced on April 24. Qt Group's Global Senior Vice President, Erik Rehn, visited Chengmai Technology's booth, where both parties engaged in in-depth discussions on core smart cockpit technologies. According to Jin10, the focus was on key applications of 3D Human-Machine Interface (HMI) in smart cockpits, including central control screens, digital instruments, AR-HUD, and ADAS visualization interactions. The discussions covered technological trends and practical experiences, with preliminary plans for potential future project collaborations.

As a significant collaboration showcase at the Beijing Auto Show, Chengmai Technology demonstrated the Outpace panoramic cockpit HMI project, jointly developed with Qt Group. This project utilizes Rendering as a Service (RaaS) and SR technologies to deliver real-time 3D rendering and real-time environmental perception visualization on mid-range, cost-effective automotive-grade chips.

data.title: Stablecoins Evolving into Global Financial Infrastructure, a16z Report Finds
data.description:
A recent study by a16z highlights the transformation of stablecoins from initial roles as transaction settlement tools and value storage to becoming a part of global financial infrastructure. According to Odaily, the report indicates that following the regulatory clarity provided by the U.S. GENIUS Act, the stablecoin market has seen accelerated growth. By the first quarter of 2026, adjusted transaction volume reached approximately $4.5 trillion.

The report reveals a 128% year-on-year increase in consumer-to-business (C2B) stablecoin transactions, totaling 284.6 million transactions. Additionally, monthly collateral deposits for stablecoin card projects surged from nearly zero at the end of 2024 to over $300 million by early 2026, reflecting the rapid expansion of stablecoin payment scenarios.

Furthermore, the velocity of stablecoin circulation has increased from 2.6 times at the beginning of 2024 to six times, indicating a shift from being held to being frequently used. a16z suggests that the focus of stablecoin applications is moving from cross-border payments to local payment infrastructure, with significant growth observed in markets such as Asia and Brazil.

data.title: a16z: Stablecoins are evolving from trading tools into core financial infrastructure, with Q1 trading volume reaching $4.5 trillion.
data.description: A new study by a16z indicates that stablecoins are evolving from initial transaction settlement tools and stores of value into global financial infrastructure. Data shows that the stablecoin market has accelerated further growth following the clarification of regulations driven by the US GENIUS Act, with adjusted transaction volume reaching approximately $4.5 trillion in the first quarter of 2026. The report shows that consumer-to-merchant (C2B) stablecoin transactions increased by 128% year-on-year, reaching 284.6 million transactions. Monthly collateralized deposits for stablecoin card projects also grew from near zero at the end of 2024 to over $300 million at the beginning of 2026, reflecting the rapid expansion of stablecoin payment scenarios. Meanwhile, the velocity of stablecoin circulation has increased from 2.6 times to 6 times since the beginning of 2024, indicating a shift from "being held" to "being frequently used." a16z believes that the focus of stablecoin applications is shifting from cross-border payments to local payment infrastructure, particularly evident in markets such as Asia and Brazil.

data.title: Whale Opens Leveraged Long Position on Brent Crude Oil
data.description: A significant market player has initiated a leveraged long position on Brent crude oil, valued at $20 million. According to NS3.AI, this move reflects a bullish sentiment towards the commodity, potentially influencing market dynamics. The position is leveraged at five times, indicating a high level of confidence in future price increases. This development may impact investor strategies and market volatility in the coming period.

data.title: Vietnam Extends Electric Vehicle Tax Incentives Until 2030
data.description: Vietnam's National Assembly approved amendments to the Special Consumption Tax Law on April 24, extending the preferential tax policy for electric vehicles with fewer than 24 seats until the end of 2030. According to Jin10, the tax rate for electric vehicles will continue to be the lowest among all vehicle categories. Previously, Vietnam reduced the special consumption tax for electric vehicles to between 1% and 3% on March 1, 2022, with the original expiration set for March 1, 2027. In contrast, the special consumption tax rate for fuel-powered vehicles ranges from 10% to 150%. Vietnam's Ministry of Transport stated that electric vehicles already benefit from the lowest tax rate in the automotive industry. Vietnam's Finance Minister, Wu Wenjun, reported to the National Assembly that extending the preferential period helps stabilize consumer expectations and aids businesses in planning medium- to long-term investments.

data.title: A whale (a cryptocurrency trader) used 5x leverage to go long on Brent crude oil, and its current position is worth $20 million.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a whale account "0x66f" has opened a long position in Brent crude oil with 5x leverage, and the current position value has reached $20 million.

data.title: Colombia and U.S. Authorities Dismantle Crypto Money Laundering Network Linked to Mexican Cartel
data.description:
Colombian and U.S. law enforcement agencies have successfully dismantled a transnational cryptocurrency money laundering network associated with Mexico's Jalisco New Generation Cartel (CJNG). According to ChainCatcher, the network has transferred over $190 million in illegal funds through cryptocurrency channels since 2023. Authorities seized multiple crypto wallets and cash during the operation.

The investigation revealed that the network employed tactics such as fund splitting, multi-wallet transfers, cross-jurisdictional movements, and token swaps to evade tracking. Specific details regarding the cryptocurrencies involved, trading platforms, and the number of arrests have not yet been disclosed, as the investigation is still ongoing.

data.title: Singapore police and cryptocurrency platforms work together to stop over S$2.86 million in fraudulent transactions in one month.
data.description: To combat cryptocurrency scams, the Singapore Police Force's Anti-Scam Centre and Cyber ​​Investigations Unit collaborated with cryptocurrency platforms in a crackdown, successfully intercepting over S$2.86 million in fraudulent funds. The Singapore Police Force stated in a press release on Thursday (April 23) that authorities, in cooperation with cryptocurrency platforms such as Coinbase, Coinhako, StraitsX, and Upbit, conducted a month-long enforcement operation from March 16 to April 15. During the operation, authorities utilized tools from blockchain intelligence company TRM Labs and blockchain analytics company Chainalysis, employing advanced blockchain analytics to identify victims who had fallen victim to scams. These scams included impersonation of government officials, investment fraud, job scams, and online romance scams. (Lianhe Zaobao)

data.title: The French National Prosecutor's Office for Organized Crime reports a surge in cryptocurrency kidnapping cases, with 88 people charged and 75 detained.
data.description: The French National Prosecutor's Office against Organized Crime (Pnaco) stated that kidnapping and unlawful detention cases involving cryptocurrency are surging. Of the 12 related cases currently under investigation, 88 people have been charged, including more than 10 minors, and another 75 are in custody awaiting trial. Prosecutor's data shows that since 2023, French law enforcement has recorded 135 such cases, with 18 in 2024, rising to 67 in 2025, and reaching 47 so far in 2026. These cases mostly involve violent kidnapping, ransom extortion, and forcing victims to transfer cryptocurrency assets. Prosecutors pointed out that some suspects have appeared in multiple cases, indicating the formation of a structured criminal network. The kidnapping of Ledger co-founder David Balland is also considered a significant indicator of escalating security risks in the French cryptocurrency industry. (Lemonde)

data.title: Analysis: The IPOs of SpaceX, OpenAI, and Anthropic may attract significant funds and impact liquidity in the BTC and crypto markets.
data.description: SpaceX secretly filed its S-1 document with the U.S. SEC earlier this month, planning an IPO with a valuation of approximately $1.75 trillion and a fundraising target of $75 billion. If it lists as expected in June, it will surpass Saudi Aramco's $29 billion fundraising record in 2019 to become the largest IPO in history. Meanwhile, OpenAI and Anthropic also plan to list in the second half of this year, with the three companies expected to attract a combined total of over $240 billion. Market analysts believe that these mega-IPOs could significantly drain liquidity from risk assets, and the crypto market is currently in the same pool of funds. Because mainstream crypto assets such as Bitcoin and Ethereum have been highly correlated with risk appetite on Nasdaq and the U.S. stock market in recent years, a large amount of capital has shifted to subscribing to tech giant IPOs, potentially weakening buying support for BTC, ETH, and altcoins. Analysts point out that Coinbase's IPO in 2021 coincided with a temporary peak in Bitcoin's price, and the SpaceX IPO may once again become a significant liquidity turning point in the crypto market. (CoinDesk)

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Chinese Gold Jewelry Prices Remain Stable
data.description: On April 25, Jin10 reported that the prices of gold jewelry in China have remained largely unchanged compared to the previous day. According to Jin10, several brands are quoting prices within the range of 1,440 yuan per gram to 1,445 yuan per gram.

data.title: Avraham Eisenberg's Wallet Activity Resurfaces Post-Mango Markets Exploit
data.description: Avraham Eisenberg's wallet has reportedly signed a new transaction following the 2022 Mango Markets exploit, which was valued at approximately $110 million. According to NS3.AI, this development comes after U.S. prosecutors appealed a not-guilty verdict against Eisenberg in January.

data.title: The Balancer attacker's associated address transferred 5,609 ETH, worth $13 million, to THORChain in the past 9 hours.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ai Yi, the address associated with the Balancer attacker transferred 5,609 ETH, worth $13 million, to THORChain in the past 9 hours. In November 2025, Balancer suffered a theft of over $116 million, and the suspects in the Aave attack are the same North Korean hacking group, Lazarus Group. Both attacks have recently been frequently using Tornado attacks for money laundering.

data.title: Balancer Attack Linked to North Korean Hackers, Funds Moved to THORChain
data.description: Analyst @ai_9684xtpa posted on X that hackers have been actively laundering funds, with the Balancer attacker transferring 5,609 ETH, valued at $13 million, to THORChain over the past nine hours. The Balancer platform suffered a theft exceeding $116 million, with suspicions pointing to the same group involved in the Aave attack, identified as the North Korean hacker organization Lazarus Group. Both incidents have seen frequent use of Tornado Cash for laundering activities.

data.title: An early Ethereum whale transferred 10,000 ETH to a multisignature address and may sell it soon.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ai Yi, an early Ethereum whale has transferred another 10,000 ETH (approximately $23.21 million) to a multi-signature address, which may be sold soon. The address receiving the tokens, 0x26c…B9392, has deposited a total of 12,001 ETH into OKX over the past two months, worth a total of $24.62 million. Based on past experience, ETH transferred to this address will subsequently flow to exchanges in batches.

data.title: Mango Markets attacker Avi Eisenberg's address reappearing with on-chain signatures has drawn community attention.
data.description: Arkham posted on the X platform that Avi Eisenberg, the attacker who profited approximately $110 million in 2022 by manipulating the Mango Markets price oracle, has recently had his on-chain address signing transactions again, drawing widespread attention from the community. Avi Eisenberg previously exploited vulnerabilities in Mango Finance to execute "high-profit trades," subsequently threatening to attack Aave, and was also arrested and imprisoned after attempting to liquidate others' positions on Curve, which resulted in his own liquidation. Now that his address has reappeared on-chain activity, the market is speculating whether he has returned to the crypto market, although no further details have been disclosed yet.
